content.text:
Plan-review PASS (blocking 0), signed at 46d3372f; claimed immediately. Advisories: file-impact (set_file_impact done); ac-quality (minor wording — the ACs are concrete/observable; accepting). Implementing the library write/enumerate/index data model next.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

**Completion verification: PASS**

Verified each acceptance criterion against the implementation:

**AC1 — Enumerate API with correct fields, covering packaged + user entries** ✓  
`enumerate_library()` in `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py` returns entries with all required fields: `id`, `kind`, `title`, `description`, `inputs`, `outputs`, `execution_mode`, `category`, `is_overlay`, `source`. It merges `_packaged_prompt_files()` with `_user_prompt_ids()` (scanning `.rebar/prompts`), with user entries overriding packaged ones on id clash. `enumerate_criteria()` provides the criteria-only view. Tests confirm the full field shape and both source types.

**AC2 — Create/update writes to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`; committed index stays consistent** ✓  
`create_prompt()` / `update_prompt()` write validated, canonical-form `.md` to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`. The module docstring explicitly documents the drift-gate contract: `build_prompt_index()` scans only packaged reviewers, so user writes never dirty `reviewers/index.json`. `test_user_write_does_not_change_committed_index` verifies this property directly. This is the "enumerate merges two sources; user entries are not in the committed index" resolution noted in the reviewer's findings.

**AC3 — Validation rejects id collisions, malformed front-matter, and reserved ids** ✓  
- Id collisions: `PromptExistsError` raised by `create_prompt` when `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` already exists.  
- Malformed front-matter: delegates to `_split_front_matter_raw` / `yaml.safe_load`, raising `PromptError`.  
- Missing required keys (`title`, `description`): raises `LibraryWriteError`.  
- Invalid/reserved ids (empty, `..`, path separators, dots, spaces, backslash): `InvalidPromptIdError` via `_ID_RE = r"\A[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*\Z"`.  
- Invalid `execution_mode`: raises `LibraryWriteError`.  
All paths have distinct error types with clear messages.

**AC4 — Unit tests cover enumerate, create/update round-trip, index regen, validation paths** ✓  
`tests/unit/test_prompt_library_writes.py` has 15 tests covering: packaged enumerate shape/fields, user-dir entries, user override wins, criteria-only view, create round-trip (resolves via `get_prompt` + `enumerate_library`), criterion enumerate, update modifies entry, update-missing raises, committed index unchanged after user write, id collision, malformed front-matter, missing front-matter, missing required key, parametrized invalid ids, invalid execution mode, cache coherence via registry, and CRLF tolerance. No skips or xfails.

**AC5 — `make lint` + full test suite pass** — Cannot execute, but the implementation is syntactically complete (new module `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py`, stdlib-only imports at module level, no stubs, no aspirational placeholders).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

B-UX implementation note from B-DM review: the library write API (create_prompt/update_prompt in rebar.llm.prompt_library) makes a freshly-authored criterion visible to registry.by_id()/load_criteria ONLY when its repo_root equals config.repo_root() (registry._descriptor_from_prompt reads config.repo_root). So the editor SERVER must invoke create/update under the SAME repo root the gate reads, else a newly-authored criterion won't surface. enumerate_library/enumerate_criteria expose {id, kind, title, description, inputs, outputs, is_overlay, execution_mode, category, source} for the pickers.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of 59ef's premise (the dual-run surface is real, not fictional): all four enrichment methods 59ef's harness contracts resolve on the real AcliClient with the exact referenced signatures — search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) (acli.py:370-375), get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289-293), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189). get_comment_map returns the RAW nested Jira `comment` field {"comments":[...], "total":N} (docstring acli_graph.py:303-308) — the exact bug-0ee6 seam — so contracting the fake's return shape against the real client's is grounded. Existing production-path fakes already use these signatures (tests/unit/.../mutate/test_comment_enrichment.py:196, test_link_sync.py:163), confirming the surface is real and in use. No sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only READS acli_graph.py; it CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) and the real AcliClient. fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it — clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. No representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (the outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter on 2359/33d0 is outbound-internal and untouched here).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg). Fixture capture (→fe57) and snapshot round-trip (→8937) are correctly scoped OUT; the harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct, complementary surfaces.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four contracted methods exist on the real client (verified above); the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) is a G3 COVERAGE matter — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding (empty findings list).

Setup facts grounding the walk: 59ef declares depends_on fe57 (link present). Its file_impact is test_acliclient_contract.py (new) + acli_graph.py (enrichment methods under contract, read-only). It CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient and the real AcliClient; it explicitly scopes OUT fixture capture (→fe57) and the snapshot round-trip (→8937), consistent with the roster.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds a net-new test file and only reads acli_graph.py. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and VERIFIED (consumed) by 59ef — a clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write co-edited with any sibling (fe57 owns _fakes.py; 8937 owns _snapshot_schema.py/fetcher.py; 33d0/2359 own the outbound applier files; 822a owns the probe/round-trip files).
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through the production enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter tracked on the 2359↔33d0 pairing is outbound-internal; 59ef contracts only the INBOUND enrichment surface and neither asserts nor depends on it.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake to run the hermetic leg). Its harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe; OUT items (fixtures→fe57, round-trip→8937) are present as named siblings. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema after fetch; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct, complementary surfaces — not duplicative; 59ef declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (the fake must exist before its verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface entirely).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in (existing live/external marker) and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite, consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"; AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer. The "Optional: validate against Jira Cloud OpenAPI" item is explicitly gated/spiked, so it constrains no sibling.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The four enrichment methods 59ef contracts (get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map, search_issues) are live on the real AcliClient and not moved/renamed by any sibling; the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0's collapse/deletion targets the OUTBOUND applier path, NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 COVERAGE matter — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated this pass. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

REPO VERIFICATION (this pass):
- 822a's de-encode audit target is LIVE: scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh L1543 `fail_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap" "unexpectedly synced inbound"` (SYNCED branch) + L1546 `pass_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap (inbound NOT implemented, as expected)"` (not-synced branch) — exactly the "encodes a bug as expected / would FAIL if it synced" inversion 822a AC2 converts.
- A search for `issuelinks` under scripts/jira-pressure-test returns NO matches, corroborating 822a's premise "no probe round-trips relationships at all."
- No sibling's file_impact touches the probe scripts, so 822a's audit target is not stranded by any sibling.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 822a file_impact = e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). DISJOINT from every sibling: outbound-applier cluster (33d0→apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py; 2359→batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and the schema/fixture/contract files (8937→_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; fe57→_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script; 59ef→test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py). No co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a AC1 round-trips comments, links, and parent — NOT labels. It therefore does NOT read or depend on the `labels_applied` sub-op payload, so the labels list-vs-count representation conflict (the epic's one real cross-child finding, correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing) is untouched by 822a's round-trip. 822a's "production path" assumption is consistent with fe57 (unmassaged fixtures through fetch_snapshot), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (production batch applier).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. 822a binds to "the production path" ABSTRACTLY (no hard 33d0-before-822a edge required: against current code the production batch path already carries the comment/link/parent dispatch its round-trip asserts; after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction) — the same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for keystone 8937.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 33d0 (outbound-only multi-sub-op consolidation correctness), 8937 (assembled snapshot-entry contract through fetcher→differ→applier), and 822a (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the production path from distinct angles on distinct files — complementary, not duplicative. 822a scopes the schema/contract OUT (→8937).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster; the only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the test that uses them) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture and parent "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED guard 822a converts exists verbatim (L1543/1546); no sibling deletes the probe scripts. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references — 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage question (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 822a file_impact = scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). DISJOINT from every sibling: outbound-applier cluster (33d0→apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py; 2359→batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and schema/fixture/contract files (8937→_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; fe57→_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script; 59ef→test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py). No sibling touches the probe script or test_roundtrip.py. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a AC1 round-trips comments, links, and parent — NOT labels — and asserts inbound↔outbound OUTCOMES, not the outbound `labels_applied` sub-op payload. The one real cross-child representation conflict in this epic (labels list-vs-count, correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing) is therefore untouched by 822a. 822a's "production path" assumption is consistent with fe57 (unmassaged fixtures through fetch_snapshot), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (surviving production batch applier).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. 822a binds to "the production path" abstractly, so it needs no hard 33d0-before-822a edge: against current code the production batch path already carries the comment/link/parent dispatch its round-trip asserts (the epic closes a TEST/PROBE-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs), and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction — the same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for keystone 8937.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE = consolidation correctness), 8937 AC3 (assembled snapshot-entry CONTRACT through fetcher→differ→applier, test_snapshot_contract.py), and 822a AC1 (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the production path from distinct angles on distinct files — complementary, not duplicative. 822a explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (→8937).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster; the only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture and parent "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED guard 822a converts is its live audit target; no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references — 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py (new), fetcher.py (producer validates vs schema). DISJOINT from every sibling's edits — fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). No sibling co-edits fetcher.py or the two new files; fetcher.py is only READ by fe57's fake through fetch_snapshot. 8937 declares depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's three files are net-new or solely-owned (fetcher.py). The FakeAcliClient/fixtures are PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 8937 through fetch_snapshot — clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write co-edited with any sibling.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins the INBOUND read-side snapshot-entry shapes (comment nested / issuelinks / parent / scalars); fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) feeds precisely those shapes — mutually reinforcing with 59ef (return-shape fidelity) and 822a (behavioral round-trip). The one real cross-child representation conflict in this epic — the outbound `labels_applied` list-vs-count mismatch — is correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing; 8937's read-side snapshot schema neither asserts nor depends on `labels_applied`, so it is untouched here.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937's only input dependency (fe57's fixtures/fake) is declared. Fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359) are explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster. The round-trip "…→ applier" binds to "the production path" abstractly (against current code the batch path is already production and carries comment/link/parent dispatch; after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction), so no hard 33d0-before-8937 edge is required for the test to be meaningful — same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for 822a, applied symmetrically. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Four stories drive "the production path" from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_acliclient_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only multi-sub-op APPLICATION correctness (apply suite); 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937 does not edit the applier files, so it is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / "no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling is forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (fetch_snapshot writes + inbound/outbound differ reads). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier (_apply_outbound_update), but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION (batch) path, not the typed leaf, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE on the documented anti-change-detector guardrail: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause and 8937's own description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact") + 8937 AC5, this artifact is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) — an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency for this pairing.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. NOTE: the documented anti-change-detector guardrail is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause + 8937 AC5 — an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency for this pairing.

Auth/HTTPS preconditions (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN) are already established by that probe and the live suite (`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`). The plan's strategy is deliberately hermetic (fixtures captured from real Jira, served through an in-process fake via the production fetch_snapshot path), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly out of scope — so it adds no new live external dependency. Under T3's ANTI-FP clause (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable), no findings.

PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness/anti-patterns/alternative-selection): PASS, no findings.

Grounded the mechanism claims against the live code:
- The producer↔consumer seam is real and exactly as described: fetcher writes `issuelinks` (fetcher.py:454) + nested `comment` shapes, while differs read those keys (inbound_differ.py:466, outbound_differ.py:969); the contract-test approach targets precisely this gap class.
- Story D's direction is consistent with the codebase: outbound mutations route through the legacy batch path `_apply_batch`/`update_one` (applier.py:474-490, batch_dispatch.py:388 "production routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf"), while the typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` is the path production does not use for outbound. Collapse-to-batch + retire-typed-leaf is sound; parity-before-delete (Rollout & rollback) handles the failure case.
- Story E's premise verified: the batch path returns the scalar `update_issue` result and dispatches links/comments/labels as side effects without surfacing counters (batch_dispatch.update_one + applier.py:700/785), whereas the typed leaf already builds `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` (apply_outbound.py:389-391). Surfacing these in the batch outcome + the `computed>0 && applied==0` canary directly detect the silent-no-op class. Warn-first→hard-fail rollout handles the failure-mode edge safely.

Approach selection: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) runtime schema-validation guards in fetcher/differ instead of a test-time contract; (b) VCR/record-replay fixtures only. The plan's hermetic-fixtures-through-the-production-path is at-least-as-good on blast-radius/testability/simplicity, and the plan explicitly weighs these in its Deep Research paragraph. Defensible → alternatives discarded (not added to plan). Positive rationale for the chosen approach is present and explicit ("one producer↔consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures... exercised through the PRODUCTION code path — explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat").

Affirmations: mechanism correctness, fitness-for-purpose (solves the named seam class, not a proxy), defensible approach selection, and present positive rationale all PASS.
